text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Helen Carter 
Consultant Neurologist  
Centre for Epilepsy  
KCH  

Dear Dr Helen Carter, 

Mr Thomas Reed, DOB: 22-09-1987, Hospital No: K843920 NHS No. 1038475629  
24 Market Road, London, SE5 9PL  

Diagnosis: 

Focal epilepsy with focal impaired awareness seizure monthly; good tolerance of current regimen 

Aetiology: 

No structural lesion identified on prior MRI; normal perinatal history; family history negative for epilepsy 

Other Medical Problems: 

Mild dyslipidaemia under primary care review; vitamin D insufficiency previously noted and repleted 

Management Plan: 

- Continue Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily.  
- Maintain current ketogenic/modified diet as supervised externally; diet details recorded in our notes for reference.  
- Routine bloods today (U&E, LFT, lipids, vitamin D) given dietary therapy.  
- Seizure diary to be continued; if any increase beyond the current pattern of focal impaired awareness seizure monthly, patient to contact clinic.  
- DVLA guidance discussed and understood.  
- Review in six months, sooner if breakthrough clusters or new features.

Since the last review, the patient reports stable focal epilepsy with a consistent pattern of focal impaired awareness seizure monthly, typically occurring in the late afternoon with brief behavioural arrest, oral automatisms, and post-event fatigue lasting 30–45 minutes. No generalised tonic–clonic seizures have occurred. There have been no injuries. Triggers noted by the patient include missed meals and poor sleep. He commenced a ketogenic/modified diet 10 months ago under the supervision of an external metabolic clinic; diet details have been recorded in our file, and he reports steady adherence without significant gastrointestinal intolerance. Weight has reduced by 4 kg over six months, which he finds acceptable. 

Medication adherence is good, with no rash, dizziness, or mood change on Lamotrigine. He declines additional antiseizure medication at present given functional stability and tolerability. We discussed options for future optimisation (e.g. slow titration of current therapy or alternative agents) should the pattern change. Safety and SUDEP risk counselling reinforced. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: consistent pattern of focal impaired awareness seizure monthly

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit frequency statements. The Diagnosis line states: 'Focal epilepsy with focal impaired awareness seizure monthly'. The Management Plan says: 'if any increase beyond the current pattern of focal impaired awareness seizure monthly'. The progress section reiterates: 'a consistent pattern of focal impaired awareness seizure monthly'. This clearly indicates approximately one seizure per month currently. No other seizure type with higher frequency is reported. No seizure-free period is indicated.
seizure_frequency_number: consistent pattern of focal impaired awareness seizure monthly
